sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the layout more clearly:
Rows 14-17, cols 20-44:
- Cols 20-25: B
- Cols 26-29: S (the left square)
- Cols 30-31: M
- Cols 32-33: p
- Cols 34-37: S (the right square)
- Cols 38-43: B
So the squares are at cols 26-29 and 34-37. To the left of the left square is B (cols 20-25), and to the right of the right square is B (cols 38-43).
If I press LEFT, the left square would move to cols 22-25 (which is B) and the right square would move to cols 38-41 (which is B). So LEFT should work!
Then from cols 22-25 and 38-41:
- LEFT again: left to 18-21, right to 42-45
- LEFT again: left to 14-17, right to 46-49
- LEFT again: left to 10-13, right to 50-53
So 4 LEFTs would get the squares to cols 10-13 and 50-53, which are the side corridors where DOWN works.
But wait, I need to check what's at rows 18-21 for the squares to move DOWN. From the ascii:
- Rows 18-21, cols 6-14: B (mostly)
- Rows 18-21, cols 48-57: ppBBBBBBB (cols 48-49 are p, cols 50-56 are B)
So at cols 10-13, rows 18-21 are B. And at cols 50-53, rows 18-21 are B. So the squares can move DOWN from rows 14-17 to rows 18-21 at cols 10-13 and 50-53.
Let me try moving LEFT 4 times to get to the side corridors, then DOWN.
Actually, wait. Let me check what's at rows 18-21, cols 22-25 and 38-41. If the squares are at rows 14-17, cols 22-25 and 38-41, can they move DOWN?
From the ascii output earlier:
- Rows 18-25, cols 24-31: M
- Rows 18-25, cols 32-41: p
So rows 18-21, cols 22-25: cols 22-23 are unknown, cols 24-25 are M.
And rows 18-21, cols 38-41: cols 38-41 are p.
So the squares can't move DOWN from cols 22-25 or 38-41 because rows 18-21 don't have B at those columns.
So the plan is: move LEFT 4 times to get to cols 10-13 and 50-53, then move DOWN.
Let me try this.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Squares at rows 14-17, cols 26-29 and 34-37
# Need to move LEFT to get to side corridors (cols 10-13 and 50-53)
# Then DOWN to move through the B corridors

# LEFT moves: 26-29 -> 22-25 -> 18-21 -> 14-17 -> 10-13 (4 LEFTs)
# And: 34-37 -> 38-41 -> 42-45 -> 46-49 -> 50-53 (4 LEFTs)

predict(expect_change=True, note='LEFT x4 - move squares to side corridors')
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Blue: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Blue: rows 14-17, cols 10-13
Blue: rows 14-17, cols 50-53
